content: Just as the high school English writing curriculum frames reflection as an iterative learning process that transforms rough draft ideas into polished writing through explicit metacognitive practice, the Reflexion autonomous agent framework can borrow the structured, scaffolded reflection routines from writing pedagogy to improve LLM self-reflection mechanisms. This would mean replacing unstructured open-ended self-reflection for LLMs with graduated reflection prompts modeled on how writing teachers teach reflection: first correcting factual errors, then refining logical flow, then aligning outputs to core goals.
